data.title: Home Depot Shares Hit 52-Week Low as Iran War Threatens Housing Rebound
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller 




  Shares of Home Depot hit a 52-week low Thursday as the conflict in Iran puts the long-awaited rebound of the housing market at risk. 



  The stock fell to $318.66 before rebounding to $320.67 a share Thursday. Shares are down 2.7% ahead of the close, and have declined close to 16% since before the beginning of the conflict in the Middle East, which has increased mortgage rates and fueled economic uncertainty, sending potential homebuyers to the sidelines. 



  Home improvement retailers including Home Depot rely on housing turnover to drive sales since homeowners often undertake remodeling projects before selling or after buying a home. 



  Home Depot has seen weak sales in recent quarters as a stagnant housing market limited home-improvement demand. The company has also said that economic uncertainty, including slipping home prices and a wobbly job market, have led homeowners to delay remodeling projects. High interest rates have particularly curtailed discretionary upgrades that homeowners typically fund with financing. 



  Mortgage rates fell in the second half of 2025 and dropped below 6% in February for the first time since 2022, sparking hopes of a rise in homebuyer demand in time for the start of the spring selling season. 



  But since the conflict in the Middle East began, mortgage rates have surged, reaching an average of 6.46% this week, according to Freddie Mac. 



  Higher gas prices have also threatened to weigh on the finances of potential homebuyers, while the prospect of an economic downturn could make buyers hesitant. 



  "If the cost of everything else is also going up, people probably don't want to add to their monthly housing costs too," said Realtor.com Senior Economist Joel Berner this week. "If this is the prevailing sentiment, we could see another lackluster spring selling season." 



  Homebuilder KB Home cut its fiscal-year guidance last month, warning that the conflict in the Middle East is creating uncertainty that could put off homebuyers. 





  Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com. 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 02, 2026 13:11 ET (17:11 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Inc. Names FlyGuys to Its 2026 List of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in the Southeast
data.description:
Lafayette-based reality data capture platform climbs from No. 97 to No. 36 in second consecutive appearance

LAFAYETTE, LA / ACCESS Newswire / April 2, 2026 / FlyGuys, the reality data platform powering industries seeking data capture, has been ranked No. 36 on the 2026 Inc. Regionals: Southeast list, the most prestigious ranking of the fastest growing privately held companies in the area. The recognition marks FlyGuys' second consecutive appearance on the list and continues the company's rapid growth trajectory, including No. 97 on the 2025 Inc. Regionals: Southeast list, and No. 1,022 on the 2025 national Inc. 5000, to No. 36 on this year's Southeast list.

FlyGuys was noted for its growth of 288% over a two-year period.

"The ranking reflects what we've been building - a national pipeline for AI that works at any scale, in any geography," said FlyGuys CEO Joe Stough. "Every year of growth is a reflection of our team, our pilot network and the enterprise customers who trust us with their most critical data capture needs."

FlyGuys serves as the infrastructure layer between the physical world and the AI and enterprise platforms that depend on real-world data. The company's network of more than 20,000 FAA-certified drone pilots executes missions using standardized workflows that ensure data consistency across thousands of locations - enabling enterprises to launch, manage and scale reality data capture programs without building internal teams or managing fragmented vendors. FlyGuys supports customers across construction, solar, agriculture, facilities inspections, insurance, real estate and public safety, among other verticals.

The company marked its seventh consecutive year of double-digit expansion, driven by enterprise demand for scalable, standardized data infrastructure.

Between 2022 and 2024, the 182 private companies on this year's Inc. Regionals: Southeast list posted a median growth rate of 89 percent, added 12,079 jobs and contributed $3.4 billion to the region's economy. The region includes South Carolina, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, Florida, and Puerto Rico.

Complete results of the Inc. Regionals: Southeast, including company profiles and an interactive database sortable by industry and metro area, are available athttps://www.inc.com/regionals/southeast.

More about Inc. and the Inc. Regionals

Methodology

The Inc. Regionals lists are ranked according to percentage revenue growth over two years. To qualify, companies must have been founded and generating revenue by March 31, 2022. They had to be U.S.-based, privately held, for-profit, and independent - not subsidiaries or divisions of other companies - as of December 31, 2024. The minimum revenue required for 2022 is $100,000; the minimum for 2024 is $1 million. As always, Inc. reserves the right to decline applicants for subjective reasons.

About Inc.

Inc. is the leading media brand and playbook for the entrepreneurs and business leaders shaping our future. Published by Mansueto Ventures LLC, along with Fast Company. For more information, visit www.inc.com.

About FlyGuys

FlyGuys is the nationwide marketplace connecting professional data capturers with clients seeking real-time intelligence solutions. Using a network of certified drone pilots and advanced technology, FlyGuys delivers actionable aerial insights for industries including telecommunications, construction, agriculture, and more. Learn more about FlyGuys at www.flyguys.com.

.

For media inquiries, contact Escalate PR on behalf of FlyGuys at FlyGuys@escalatepr.com

SOURCE: FlyGuys

data.title: Forest Road Launches CenterNode, with up to $750 Million in Capital Commitments from Institutional Investors, including Liberty Mutual Investments
data.description:
Forest Road Launches CenterNode, with up to $750 Million in Capital Commitments from Institutional Investors, including Liberty Mutual Investments

Seth Zeleznik, veteran investment executive and founder of CenterNode, leads the platform to finance U.S. renewable energy development

The Forest Road Company ("Forest Road") today announced the launch of CenterNode Group ("CenterNode"), its dedicated alternative energy investment arm, alongside up to $750 million in initial capital commitments from institutional investors, including Liberty Mutual Investments (“LMI”), the investment firm for Liberty Mutual. LMI’s commitment will support the scaling of CenterNode’s platform as it addresses the widening gap in the U.S. solar and infrastructure market. Seth Zeleznik, a 20-year veteran of private credit, private equity, and sustainable infrastructure, serves as CEO and Founder of CenterNode and will lead the platform's investment strategy and day-to-day operations.

CenterNode is an opportunistic energy and infrastructure investment platform built on a simple conviction: the transition to energy independence demands a new kind of capital partner. The platform aims to deploy flexible capital across the capital structure and the alternative energy ecosystem, including developers, projects, and assets ranging from $5 million to $50 million. CenterNode will initially deploy capital across U.S. solar and storage, with plans to expand into additional energy and infrastructure subsectors as it scales.

"The U.S. energy transition is entering its most capital-intensive phase at precisely the moment when traditional lenders have stepped back," said Seth Zeleznik, CEO and Founder of CenterNode. "We built CenterNode to be the flexible, institutional-grade capital partner that developers and project owners need right now—one that can move quickly, structure creatively, and execute with certainty."

LMI invests more than $124 billion of long-term capital globally across its integrated platform on behalf of Liberty. As a long-term, partnership-oriented investor, LMI works side by side with sponsors and operators to build enduring businesses, providing flexible capital and tailored solutions to support partners across market cycles. The firm’s Alternative Credit platform provides bespoke asset financing solutions across the capital structure and public and private markets, targeting opportunities where it can be a differentiated solutions provider.

“The energy market is at a pivotal moment, with power demand accelerating while available financing has failed to keep pace,” said Charley Poole, Head of Energy & Infrastructure at Liberty Mutual Investments. “Meeting that demand will require significant capital to accelerate development across the energy landscape, creating opportunities where LMI can be a constructive capital partner to infrastructure platforms like CenterNode.”

“We believe CenterNode is well-positioned against this current backdrop to deliver flexible, solutions-oriented capital to solar developers navigating an increasingly complex financing landscape,” said John Kim, Head of Alternative Credit at Liberty Mutual Investments. “We’re excited to partner with CenterNode and leverage our long-term capital and integrated investment platform to help scale the business.”

About Seth Zeleznik and the CenterNode Leadership

Seth Zeleznik, who serves as CEO and Founder of CenterNode, brings 20 years of experience across private credit, private equity, and sustainable infrastructure. Most recently, he advised Apollo Global Management's Sustainable Infrastructure Group following its acquisition of Irradiant Partners, where he served on the Management Committee and Investment Committees for renewables private equity, sustainable infrastructure credit, and opportunistic credit funds. Prior to that, Seth held senior roles as a Managing Director at Blackstone and as a Senior Managing Director at Kayne Anderson.

Zachary Tarica, who serves as Chairman of CenterNode and CEO of Forest Road, previously led Brookfield Asset Management's strategic renewables investments, including the landmark $7.9 billion acquisition of the TerraForm companies out of the SunEdison bankruptcy—one of the most significant renewable energy transactions in history.

"The energy transition is the most consequential infrastructure build-out this country has undertaken in decades. Getting the financing right—especially at the small and mid-market level—is not just a business opportunity. It is essential to keeping the U.S. competitive," said Tarica.

About CenterNode Group

CenterNode Group (“CNG”) is Forest Road's dedicated alternative energy investment platform, providing flexible, structured capital solutions to projects, developers, and operating companies across the power and infrastructure ecosystem. CNG operates as a relying adviser under Forest Road Asset Management, LLC, an SEC-registered investment adviser. For more information, visit www.thecenternode.com.

About The Forest Road Company

The Forest Road Company ("Forest Road") is an opportunistic investment firm focused on Media, Renewable Energy, and Digital Assets. The firm deploys capital across public and private markets through its balance sheet, asset management platform, and advisory capabilities, pursuing special situations where flexibility, speed, and sector expertise create a distinct advantage. For more information, visit www.forestroadco.com.

About Liberty Mutual Investments

Liberty Mutual Investments (“LMI”) is the investment firm for Liberty Mutual Group (“Liberty”), a global insurance and capital solutions partner. With deep expertise in liquid, credit, and alternative strategies, LMI invests more than $124B of capital globally, taking a long-term approach across its integrated platform. LMI has a clear purpose: build enduring businesses side-by-side with our partners, drive economic growth, and generate superior risk-adjusted returns that power Liberty’s strategy and secure its promises.

Liberty Mutual Group Asset Management Inc. does business under the name Liberty Mutual Investments. For more information, visit https://www.libertymutualinvestments.com/.

Media Contact
media@forestroadco.com

data.title: Corvias Launches $11 Million Resilient Flooring Program Across Six U.S. Army Installations
data.description:
Corvias Launches $11 Million Resilient Flooring Program Across Six U.S. Army Installations

Responds to resident feedback with multi-year plan to upgrade nearly 1,000 military homes

Corvias, together with its Army partners, today announced it is investing over $11 million in a resilient flooring program, replacing existing carpet with durable, hard surface flooring in nearly 1,000 military family homes. The six-year modernization effort at Aberdeen Proving Ground, Fort Rucker, Fort Sill, Fort Meade, Fort Riley, and Fort Bragg is designed to extend property longevity, reduce maintenance costs, and provide improved housing to Soldiers and their families.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260402729135/en/

New resilient flooring installed in a Junior Non-Commissioned Officer home at Fort Meade enhances durability, comfort, and long-term performance supporting quality of life for Soldiers and their families.

Resilient flooring provides improved aesthetics, increased comfort, and sustainability. The initiative directly responds to one of the top areas for improvement requested by residents in annual surveys across Corvias communities.

“Listening to our residents and acting on what we hear is fundamental to how we manage our communities,” said Kolby Stobbe, SVP, Property Management, Corvias. “This resilient flooring program improves day-to-day living for military families while strengthening long-term performance across the portfolio.”

The work is being executed in parallel with prior flooring upgrades underway through Corvias’ $210 million Homestead project. In addition to flooring, that project is improving over 2,000 homes through medium and major renovations. Both initiatives utilize the same resilient flooring product, reflecting Corvias’ long-term intent to standardize flooring throughout its portfolio.

The new flooring reduces hiding places for potential allergens while supporting easier and more effective cleaning. The program aligns with and complements Corvias’ expanding Wellness at Home program, which delivers enhanced indoor environments for military families through science-backed technologies.

Army leaders approved the expansion and rapid rollout of the Corvias Wellness at Home initiative at Fort Bragg, Fort Meade, and Fort Riley to over 4,500 residences last year, delivering further in-home upgrades including water filtration, air purification, and excess humidity controls. Those efforts have led to hundreds of Corvias homes achieving WELL for Residence certification, a first for military housing.

The comprehensive modernization efforts underway across the Corvias portfolio reflect the company’s Installation of the Future vision, where military installations serve as platforms for resilience, operational excellence, and the total care of the force. Together, these improvements help optimize installation readiness and performance aligned to long-term Department of War (DOW) and Army goals.

About Corvias

Corvias partners with the Department of War (DOW) and higher education institutions to solve infrastructure and energy resiliency challenges and to create long-term, sustainable value through our unique Solutions Through Partnerships® approach. Corvias partnerships support energy resilience and quality of indoor environments through renewable energy projects, energy savings projects, and WELL for Residential certified enhancements in homes across our communities. Our more than 72,000 residents consistently highly rank the courtesy and professionalism of our maintenance and leasing personnel. Learn more at: www.corvias.com.

Corvias Media Contact
Communications@Corvias.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260402729135/en/

data.title: Microsoft Releases AI Models for Transcription, Voice and Image Generation
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan 




  Microsoft unveiled three new artificial intelligence models offering speech-to-text transcription as well as voice and image generation. 



  The software giant said Thursday it's working to deploy the models to power its consumer and commercial products, and they are now available for its Foundry customers. 



  One of the new models, MAI-Transcribe-1, offers speech-to-text transcription across 25 languages. The model transcribes more than two times faster than Microsoft's existing Azure Fast offering, the company said. 



  The company's MAI-Voice-1 offering, meanwhile, aims to generate natural, realistic speech. Foundry users will also be able to create their own custom voice using a few seconds of audio. 



  Microsoft's image generation model, MAI-Image-2, is already in use across some enterprise partners including marketing and communications firm WPP, Microsoft said. The model allows users to generate images quickly with natural lighting, accurate skin tones and textures, the company said. 



  Microsoft has faced stumbling blocks in the race for dominance in AI. The company's Copilot chatbot, a product central to its AI strategy, hasn't won over users as a clear ChaptGPT alternative, and Wall Street has grown concerned that growth in its most important business unit, the Azure cloud-computing business, is slowing. Microsoft continues to double down on its AI efforts, with plans to invest billions globally in AI computing as demand booms. 





  Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 02, 2026 12:50 ET (16:50 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: MAPEI’s Luigi Di Geso Receives NTCA’s 2026 Joe A. Tarver Award at Coverings
data.description:
MAPEI’s Luigi Di Geso Receives NTCA’s 2026 Joe A. Tarver Award at Coverings

Recognition followed Gary Sinise keynote address sponsored by MAPEI Corporation

MAPEI Corporation announced today that Luigi Di Geso, President and CEO of MAPEI North America, has been awarded the National Tile Contractors Association’s (NTCA) prestigious Joe A. Tarver Service to the Industry Award for 2026. Di Geso was formally honored during Coverings 2026, following the Gary Sinise keynote address – an event that was proudly sponsored by MAPEI.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260402359033/en/

MAPEI North America's President and CEO Luigi Di Geso receives the Joe A. Tarver Award from NTCA's Executive Director Bart Bettiga at Coverings 2026.

Presented annually to one U.S.-based industry professional, the Joe A. Tarver Award recognizes individuals whose careers reflect extraordinary service, leadership and long-term impact on the tile and stone industry. Named after NTCA Executive Director Emeritus Joe Tarver, the award honors those who have strengthened the industry through advocacy, education and collaboration.

In announcing the award, Bart Bettiga, Executive Director of NTCA, praised Di Geso’s decades of commitment to the trade, stating: "The Joe Tarver Award is given to an individual who positively impacts the tile industry throughout their career. As the leader of MAPEI North America, Luigi Di Geso has left an indelible footprint on the tile and stone trade. MAPEI supports the NTCA and the entire industry in our effort to grow the tile market. MAPEI is a leader in training and education and through sponsorship and association support, they play a leading role in the proper specification of tile-installation materials, resulting in successful installations. Luigi and the entire team at MAPEI are deeply appreciated by the members and leaders of the National Tile Contractors Association and we are honored to recognize his accomplishments."

"I am deeply honored to receive this recognition from NTCA,” Di Geso said. “I love this industry and the people who make it what it is. My goal has always been to strengthen professionalism, education and collaboration among manufacturers, associations, distributors and contractors. When individuals grow, the entire industry grows – and that has guided my career every day."

Di Geso has been a member of the MAPEI family for more than two decades. From 2004 to 2009, he served as General Manager of MAPEI’s Canadian subsidiary before being appointed President and CEO of MAPEI North America. Under his leadership, the company has expanded from three product lines to 19, while increasing its operational footprint from 12 to 28 facilities across North America and the Caribbean.

During Di Geso’s tenure, MAPEI North America’s revenue has grown from $260 million to more than $1 billion annually, reflecting a strategy centered on innovation, sustainability and long-term investment. Di Geso has championed the development of environmentally responsible technologies that support circular construction practices and low-impact manufacturing.

Beyond MAPEI, Di Geso remains deeply engaged in advancing the broader construction industry, collaborating with more than 80 professional organizations that include the Tile Council of North America (TCNA), Greater Miami Chamber of Commerce, Italy-America Chamber of Commerce Southeast, Italian Chamber of Commerce in Canada and the Italian-Canadian Community Foundation.

Di Geso’s leadership emphasizes cross-functional excellence, integrating research and development, marketing, sales, customer service and technical services to deliver solutions that contractors can trust – reinforcing MAPEI’s reputation as a long-term partner for professionals across the building sector.

Di Geso received the Joe A. Tarver Award during the NTCA/TCNA Awards Program at Coverings 2026, which was held on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

About MAPEI

Founded in 1937 in Milan, MAPEI is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of chemical products for the building industry and has contributed to the construction of some of the most important architectural and infrastructure works worldwide. With 98 subsidiaries operating in 59 countries and 106 manufacturing plants in 42 nations, MAPEI Group employs about 13,200 staff worldwide. The foundations for the success of the company are specialization, internationalization, research & development, and sustainability.

Headquartered in Deerfield Beach (Florida), MAPEI North America consists of the subsidiaries MAPEI Corporation (for the USA), MAPEI Inc. (for Canada) and MAPEI Caribe (for Puerto Rico and other Caribbean islands), whose 28 facilities collectively have a workforce of about 2,000 employees. MAPEI facilities are certified to be in conformance with requirements in the ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 Standards for Management Systems. An environmentally conscious manufacturer that reinvests a percentage of its revenue into R&D, MAPEI offers training for architects, contractors, installers and distributors through the MAPEI Technical Institute. For more about MAPEI, visit www.mapei.com or call 1-800-42-MAPEI (1-800-426-2734).

MAPEI NORTH AMERICA – PRESS AND PUBLIC RELATIONS OFFICE Tel. 954-246-8793 – jkramer@mapei.com – www.mapei.com

data.title: Linde to Build New Air Separation Plant in North Carolina
data.description:
Linde to Build New Air Separation Plant in North Carolina

Linde announced today that it will build, own and operate a new facility for the supply of industrial gases in Garysburg, North Carolina.

The new air separation unit (ASU) will produce liquid oxygen, nitrogen, and argon, serving customers in the region. Acquisition of land and equipment for the new site has begun and the plant is expected to start up at the end of 2028.

“We are experiencing incredible growth across multiple end markets in the area,” said Todd Lawson, Vice President East Region, Linde. “This investment will add to our already robust production and supply network, to meet the current and future industrial gas needs of customers in the region.”

About Linde

Linde is a leading global industrial gases and engineering company with 2025 sales of $34 billion. We live our mission of making our world more productive every day by providing high-quality solutions, technologies and services which are making our customers more successful and helping to sustain, decarbonize and protect our planet.

Linde serves a variety of end markets such as chemicals & energy, food & beverage, electronics, healthcare, manufacturing, metals and mining. Linde’s industrial gases and technologies are used in countless applications, enabling space exploration and launch technologies, delivering ultra-high-purity and specialty gases for semiconductor manufacturing, providing life-saving medical oxygen and enabling clean hydrogen production and carbon capture to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. Linde also delivers state-of-the-art gas processing solutions to support customer growth, efficiency improvements and emissions reductions.

For more information about the company and its products and services, please visit www.linde.com

Media Relations
Leslie Agee
Phone : 918.960.1721
Email : leslie.agee@linde.com

data.title: How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation
data.description:
How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation

How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation

PR Newswire

PLYMOUTH, Ind., April 2, 2026

PLYMOUTH, Ind., April 2, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- What should you know before a tent rental in Plymouth? A HelloNation article provides a clear look at how local weather, site conditions, and regional rules shape the tent rental process for events throughout northern Indiana. The piece, written for readers across the Michiana region, highlights how early planning, communication with vendors, and awareness of policies can make all the difference for a successful event.

In the feature, Daniel Tiser, a Rental Expert based in Plymouth and owner of Michiana Rental, helps readers understand why preparation is the single most important factor in tent rental success. According to the HelloNation article, renters who account for weather changes and site challenges avoid the kinds of last-minute problems that often cause stress during setup. The article explains that Plymouth sits in a part of Indiana where weather conditions can change quickly, meaning rental companies often adjust schedules to meet narrow delivery windows.

The tent rental process, as Tiser describes it, begins long before the tent is raised. Many clients start with tent size, but he notes that the condition of the ground is just as important. Different surfaces—grass, gravel, or pavement—require different staking or ballasting methods, which can alter both cost and setup time. The HelloNation article points out that local companies often request photos or even site visits before confirming a booking to check for underground utilities or soft areas that could make installation unsafe.

For residents across the Michiana region, these steps may feel tedious, but they prevent bigger issues later. In Plymouth, unpredictable rainfall and wind make it essential to plan for solid footing and quick drainage around tents. Daniel Tiser explains that understanding these local conditions helps clients avoid last-minute delays that can affect an entire event schedule. By focusing on preparation, renters not only protect their investment but also ensure a smoother experience for everyone involved.

Tent rental in the Michiana region also involves understanding local permit requirements. The HelloNation article notes that larger tents, especially those placed near public spaces or sidewalks, may need special approvals from the city. These permits are often tied to fire safety codes, requiring specific exit layouts or restrictions on sidewalls. Clients who wait too long to apply may face rushed timelines or unexpected setup limits. Tiser's advice is to check with local authorities well before the event date to prevent installation delays.

Hidden costs also play a major role in the rental experience. The article explains that delivery fees, cleaning charges, and fuel surcharges often appear in contracts but are easy to overlook. During peak months, when crews travel across county lines, these fees can add up quickly. Plymouth's narrow access routes and soft lawns can further complicate delivery, leading to additional charges for labor or equipment. The HelloNation piece emphasizes reading every line of the rental contract carefully to identify such costs early.

Another key point from the article is how weather impacts cancellation and rescheduling terms. In the Michiana region, where storms can arrive without warning, some companies offer partial refunds or credit toward future rentals, while others limit flexibility once crews are en route. Tiser underscores the value of reviewing these policies in advance to avoid disappointment when severe weather makes setup unsafe. By planning around local weather patterns, clients can make informed decisions about timing, protection, and event layout.

Beyond tent rental, the HelloNation article extends its insights to tool and equipment rental across Plymouth and surrounding towns. Homeowners renting generators, lifts, or compactors are advised to review fuel and cleaning rules, as these often influence return conditions and fees. Many rental companies, including Michiana Rental, provide short safety demonstrations before each rental. Tiser encourages renters to ask questions about proper operation and storage to avoid damage or additional charges.

Through examples and practical advice, the article reinforces that communication is the key to a successful rental experience. Whether the project involves an outdoor wedding, a community event, or a home repair job, having a clear checklist—covering site readiness, permits, weather planning, and contract terms—helps prevent confusion and delay. As Tiser notes, when clients approach tent rental with clear expectations, both the renter and provider benefit from a smoother, more predictable process.

In Plymouth and throughout the Michiana region, tent rental is not just about picking the right tent—it's about preparation, coordination, and understanding how local conditions influence every step. By applying the guidance shared in the HelloNation article, renters can approach their next event with greater confidence and fewer surprises.

What Should You Know Before a Tent Rental in Plymouth features insights from Daniel Tiser, Rental Expert of Plymouth, Indiana, in HelloNation.

About HelloNation
HelloNation is a premier media platform that connects readers with trusted professionals and businesses across various industries. Through its innovative "edvertising" approach that blends educational content and storytelling, HelloNation delivers expert-driven articles that inform, inspire, and empower. Covering topics from home improvement and health to business strategy and lifestyle, HelloNation highlights leaders making a meaningful impact in their communities.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/how-rental-expert-daniel-tiser-of-plymouth-explains-tent-rental-preparation-for-hellonation-302733072.html

SOURCE HelloNation
